OFFICE TO LOCOMOTIVE INTERFACE 
1.1 Scope 
This document contains messages passed between the Positive Train Control (PTC) Office Segment and the Locomotive Segment , and 
messages passed between the Wayside Status Relay Service (WSRS) and the Locomotive Segment . 
1.2 Message Format 
Each message exchanged between the Office Segment/WSRS and Locomotive Segment consists of an application -layer header / wrapper which 
encapsulates the message payload data and includes a data check value.  The Edge Message Protocol (EMP) is utilized as the application -layer 
wrapper.  Segmentation of PTC messages exchanged between Office Segment/WSRS and Locomotive Segment is described in Table 1. 
Table 1 PTC Office Segment/WSRS-Locomotive Segment Message 
Field Size (Bytes) Description 
Application Message Header Variable EMP Header 
Message Body Variable Message payload.  See message definitions 
Data Integrity Value 4 EMP Data Integrity Value

PTC Office-Locomotive Segment ICD 
Release 3.1  10/20/2017 
Distribution limited to the Association of American Railroads, its direct contractors and member railroads. 
-11- 
1.3 PTC Edge Message Protocol Use Conventions 
Conventions for use of specific fields within the EMP wrapper by PTC applications are described in Table 2. 
Table 2 EMP Use Conventions for PTC Applications 
Field Size 
(Bytes) 
Description 
Protocol Version 1 Version of EMP header 
Message Type (ID) 2 As noted in the definition of each message in this ICD 
Message Version 1 As noted in the definition of each message in this ICD 
Flags 1 Timestamp Format: UTC 
No encryption 
No compression 
Data Integrity: Application-specific data integrity value used 
Data Length 3 As noted in the definition of each message in this ICD 
Message Number 4 Application Message Sequence Number 
Message Time 4 UTC timestamp of message creation by sending application 
Variable Header Size 1 Defined by length of source and destination addresses 
Time to Live 2 As defined in section 1.10 Quality of Service and Time to Live 
Routing QoS 2 As defined in section 1.10 Quality of Service and Time to Live 
Source 64 max See section 1.3.2 EMP Source and Destination Fields 
Destination 64 max See section 1.3.2 EMP Source and Destination Fields 
Data Integrity 4 Truncated Keyed Hashed Message Authentication Code.  See section 1.3.3 EMP Data Integrity Field

-12- 
1.3.1 Message Number Field 
The Message Number Field serves as a correlation ID  which assists the Office  Segment/WSRS and Locomotive Segment in associating a 
response with an initial message.  Specific requirements for populating the field are included in the on -board and office segment requirements. 
1.3.2 EMP Source and Destination Fields 
The source and destination address format and examples are defined in Appendix A of the EMP Specification  (AAR MSRP Section K -IV, Office 
Architecture and Railroad Electronics Messaging, effective August 2012). 
1.3.3 EMP Data Integrity Field 
HMACs for PTC messages between the locomotive and the BOS and messages between the locomotive and WSRS are generated using SHA-1-
160 algorithm and the Locomotive OPK.  The result of the SHA -1-160 HMAC algorithm is a 160 -bit value that is then truncated to 32 -bits for 
insertion in the PTC messages.  This calculation and truncation is covered in the FIPS-198 and NIST 800-107 publications. 
The keyed HMAC covers the EMP header fields and application message payload defined in this ICD.

1.4 Message Data 
When fields are comprised of multi-byte quantities the byte order is presumed to be most significant byte first.  For example, if a data quantity is 
represented in two bytes as a binary value of 259; the byte order for this field (as byte n and n+1) as it would appear in th e message byte-stream 
is: 
….. 
2-byte quantity 
byte n 1   (msb) 
byte n+1 3   (lsb) 
….. 
The same convention applies to multi-byte quantities of other lengths. 
Unless otherwise noted, all numeric fields in this document are unsigned.

1.5.1 L2I1 (01000) Verify Employee Info Response – Version 3 
Description:  This message is sent to the Locomotive to report the verification status of employee data sent to the Office in the Verify Employee 
Info Request (02000) message. 
Functional Content: 
Field Size (Bytes) Type Description 
Railroad SCAC 4 Printable 
ASCII 
Railroad SCAC of the railroad to which the employee information 
verification request was sent, left justified space filled 
Verification Status 1 Enumeration 0 – Not used 
1 – Employee info verified 
2 – Employee info not verified 
Employee Identifier Length 1 Numeric String length of the Employee Identifier up to 8 characters 
Employee Identifier Variable Printable 
ASCII 
Employee identifier 
Employee First Name Size 1 Numeric Size of employee first name up to 20 characters 
Employee First Name Variable Printable 
ASCII 
Employee first name 
Employee Middle Initial Size 1 Numeric Size of employee middle initial up to 1 character 
Employee Middle Initial Variable Printable 
ASCII 
Employee middle initial 
Employee Last Name Size 1 Numeric Size of employee last name up to 32 characters 
Employee Last Name Variable Printable 
ASCII 
Employee Last Name 
Size of Reason Text 1 Numeric Size of text following up to 45 characters 
Reason Text Variable Printable 
ASCII 
Text describing reason employee info was not verified 
 
[Constraints] 
[Unique error handling requirements] 
[Design notes] 
If ‘Verification Status’ is not ‘Employee info not verified’ the ‘Size of Reason Text’ is populated with zero. 
If ‘Verification Status’ is not ‘Employee info verified’, the ‘Employee First Name Size’, ‘Employee Middle Initial Size’ and ‘Employee Last Name 
Size’ are populated with zero.

1.5.5 L2I5 (01005) Configuration Version List – Version 4 
Description: Sent from the Office to the Locomotive to provide information on the Locomotive configuration versions.  This message is sent in 
response to the Configuration Version List Request (02005) message.

Field Size (Bytes) Type Description 
Railroad SCAC 4 Printable 
ASCII

list request was sent, left justified space filled

change in the train subdivision/district list to distribute to the Locomotive.  The Locomotive confirms receipt of an unsolicited message with the 
Confirmation of Train Subdivision/District List (02008) message.

Field Size (Bytes) Type Description 
Reason for Sending 1 Enumeration 0 – Not used 
1 – Solicited 
2 – Unsolicited 
 
THIS FIELD IS NOT INCLUDED IN THE CRC 
PTC Confirmation Subdivision / District ID 2 Numeric PTC Subdivision / District ID to which the Confirmation of Train

(0 -> 65535) see design notes below

ASCII

subdivisions/districts, left justified space filled

up to 99 
For each PTC Subdivision / District   Repeated as per ‘Number of PTC Subdivisions / Districts’

Field Size (Bytes) Type Description 
Railroad SCAC 4 Printable 
ASCII 
Railroad SCAC of the railroad to which the poll registration was 
sent, left justified space filled 
Reason for Sending 1 Enumeration 0 – Not Used 
1 – Confirm receipt of valid poll registration 
2 – NAK – invalid subdivision/district list 
3 – NAK – invalid subdivision/district ID 
4 – Reserved 
5 – NAK – Other 
6 – NAK – office does not support poll registration for ancillary 
subdivisions/districts

1.5.11 L2I11 (01021) Office Segment Poll – Version 5 
Description: Sent by the Office to the Locomotive to provide information on the current set of mandatory directives and track data for one or more 
subdivisions / districts and the status of control points for which the Locomotive has registered within those subdivisions / districts.  The 
Locomotive registers for polls for a specific subdivision / district with the Poll Registration (02020) message. 
Functional Content: 
Field Size (Bytes) Type Description 
Railroad SCAC 4 Printable 
ASCII 
Railroad SCAC of the railroad to which the poll registration was 
sent, left justified space filled 
Train Subdivision/District  List CRC 4 CRC-32 CRC calculated over the train subdivision/district list received in 
the Train Subdivision/District List message 
Configuration Version List CRC 4 CRC-32 CRC calculated over the list of configuration versions in the 01005 
message 
Number of PTC Subdivisions / Districts 1 Numeric Number of PTC subdivisions or districts up to 99 
For each PTC Subdivision / District   Repeated as per ‘Number of PTC Subdivisions / Districts’ 
PTC Subdivision / District ID 2 Numeric PTC Subdivision / district identifier (1 -> 65535) 
Office state 1 Enumeration 0 – Not used 
1 – Explicit 
2 – Non-explicit 
3 – Synchronizing 
4 – No CAD support 
Dataset CRC 4 CRC-32 CRC calculated over track data and all mandatory directive 
datasets and text for the subdivision / district which are expected 
to be onboard 
IC3 Uniqueness Index 1 Numeric An ever-changing value that is included in the Composite CRC 
Composite CRC 4 CRC-32 CRC calculated over IC3 Authority CRCs and IC3 Bulletin CRCs 
 
[Constraints] 
[Unique error handling requirements] 
[Design notes] 
When the ‘Office state’ field is set to ‘3 – Synchronizing’, the ‘Dataset CRC’ will be set to zero for that subdivision / district. 
When the ‘Office state’ field is set to ‘4 – No CAD support’, the ‘Dataset CRC’ will be calculated over a null list of movement authorities and 
bulletins for that subdivision/district. 
If the message contains only subdivisions/districts with the ‘Office state’ field set to ‘4 – No CAD support’, the ‘Train Subdivision/District List CRC’ 
will be set to zeroes.

The ‘Configuration Version List CRC’ field is populated with the Configuration Version List CRC calculated in the 01005 message. 
The table below shows the fields over which the ‘Dataset CRC’ is calculated in order. 
 
Field Size 
(Bytes) 
Type Description 
Repeated for each movement authority stored by the office for the locomotive to which it is sending the 
01021 message 
  Note A 
PTC Authority Reference Number 4 Numeric Note B 
CRC around Authority Dataset 4 CRC-32  
Repeated for each bulletin stored by the office for the locomotive to which it is sending the 01021 
message 
   
PTC Bulletin Reference Number 4 Numeric Note C 
CRC around Bulletin Dataset 4 CRC-32  
For the track data for the subdivision/district    
Track Data Version 4 Numeric  
CRC around Track Data 4 CRC-32  
Note A: The on-board segment calculates a Dataset CRC over the datasets it has stored in the same order.  
Note B: The authority datasets are ordered from least to greatest ‘PTC Authority Reference Number’.  When there is more than one movement 
authority with the same PTC Authority Reference Number, the original authority is first, followed by the modified authority.  
Note C: The bulletin datasets are ordered from least to greatest ‘PTC Bulletin Reference Number’ . 
The composite CRC is calculated as described in the IC3 CRC Definitions ICD Addendum document.  
If IC3 functionality is not present in the office, IC3 Uniqueness Index and Composite CRC are populated with zeroes.

Description: Sent to the Locomotive to provide the current list of mandatory directives and track data that should be onboard for a given 
subdivision / district.  This message is sent in response to the Request Current Dataset List (02022) message. 
Functional Content:

The ‘Displayable Bulletin Reference Number’ field contains the original bulletin referenc e number assigned to the bulletin by CAD or another back

-37- 
For PTC grade crossing bulletins, a 0 mph speed restriction indicates an activation failure and a non -zero speed restriction indicates a false 
activation. 
For bulletins other than of type Critical Alert, the ‘Time to Comply’ field is populated with zero. 
For bulletins of type ‘Advisory / Cautionary’, the ‘Number of Bulletin Segments’ is set to zero.  
For bulletins which do not include a start time, the ‘Effective Time Stamp’ field is populated as a zero. 
For bulletins which do not include an end time, the ‘Expire Time Stamp’ field is populated as a zero. 
For bulletins which are always in effect between the start time and the end time, the ‘Number of Time of Day Filte rs’ is populated as a zero. 
For a bulletin which is not continuously in effect, but in effect within a time range on every day of the week, the ‘Number o f Time of Day Filters’ is 
set to 7 and a separate Time of Day Filter is included for each day. 
When the ‘Time of Day End Time Stamp’ is set to an earlier time than the ‘Time of Day Start Time Stamp’ for a Time of Day Filter, the ‘Time of Day 
End Time Stamp’ is applied to the day following the ‘Time of Day Filter Day of Week’ specified for that Time of Day F ilter.  This allows a restriction 
that spans midnight to be specified with a single Time of Day filter. 
For bulletins at a single point, the ‘Segment Starting Milepost’ and ‘Segment Ending Milepost’ fields are populated with the same location. 
For bulletins where the ‘Restricted Speed’ field is set to 1 (Yes), the on-board segment will enforce the lower of the non-zero value in the ‘Speed’ 
field or the configurable value stored on-board.  If the ‘Speed’ field is populated with zero, the on-board segment will enforce the configurable 
value stored on-board. 
For bulletins other than of type ‘Speed Restriction’, the ‘Direction field is populated with ‘1 – Enforceable in both directions’. 
For bulletins for which a direction is not an attribute in the message from  Dispatch the ‘Direction’ field is populated with ‘1 – Enforceable in both 
directions’. 
A bulletin may be defined with a track designation of “ALL” if the starting milepost is the same as the ending milepost and that milepost exists on 
every track over which the bulletin is to be applied.  Otherwise, each track name must be listed in a separate bulletin segment.  
When the bulletin track designation is “ALL”, the “ALL” Track Flag field is set to true and the ‘Segment Track Name Length’ field is set to zero.  
When the bulletin track designation is not “ALL”, the “ALL” Track Flag field is set to false.  
For bulletins which do not specify a crossing by DOT ID, the ‘DOT ID’ field is populated with spaces (0x20). 
For bulletins which specify a crossing by DOT ID, the ‘Number of Bulletin Segments’ is set to zero.  Only bulletins of type ‘Grade crossing’ may be 
specified with a DOT ID.

The ‘Body of Summary Text’ field contains one line of text to be displayed as part of a list on the onboard display.  
For ‘Bulletin Type’ of ‘5 – Speed Restriction’, ‘6 – Work Zone’, ‘7 – Grade Crossing’, ‘8 – Track out of Service’ and ‘9 – Advisory / Cautionary’, the 
summary text is constructed as shown below: 
If only one bulletin line item may be assigned per bulletin number: 
AAAAAA BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB 
 
If multiple bulletin line items may be assigned per bulletin number: 
AAAAAA Ln LL BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB 
Where AAAAAA is the bulletin number provided by CAD or other railroad system , Ln is the text string “Ln”, LL is the bulletin line item number and 
BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB is the bulletin type.  The bulletin type is populated with the following static text:  
 
Type of Text Static Text 
5 – Speed Restriction SPEED RESTRICTION 
6 – Work Zone WORK ZONE 
7 – Grade Crossing GRADE CROSSING 
8 – Track out of Service TRACK OUT OF SERVICE 
9 – Advisory / Cautionary ADVISORY / CAUTIONARY 
12 – PTC Suspension PTC SUSPENSION 
The AAAAAA field and LL field are right justified.  The BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB field is left justified.  
For ‘Type of Text’ of ‘10 – Critical alert’, the summary text is constructed as shown below: 
SCAC AAAAAAAAAA 
Where SCAC is the SCAC code of the railroad issuing the critical alert and AAAAAAAAAA is the critical authority number provid ed by CAD or 
other railroad system. 
The SCAC field is right justified.  The AAAAAAAAAA field is left justified.

1.5.41 L2I31 (01102) File Part Checksum – Version 2 
Description: This message is sent by the Office to provide information on the checksum over a portion of a file that is avail able for transfer to aid 
the locomotive in resuming download of the file.  It is sent in response to the File Part Checksum Request (02102) message. 
Functional Content: 
Field Size (Bytes) Type Description 
Railroad SCAC 4 Printable 
ASCII 
Railroad SCAC of the railroad to which the the file part checksum 
request was sent, left justified space filled 
Transaction ID 1 Numeric Transaction identifier from the File Part Checksum Request 
message 
Checksum Count 1 Numeric Number of checksums (0 -> 8) 
Repeated for each Checksum    
Byte Count 4 Numeric Number of bytes that the MD5 sum covers starting at byte 0 
MD5 16 Numeric MD5 sum over the data (bytes 0 – Byte Count bytes) 
 
[Constraints] 
[Unique error handling requirements] 
[Design notes] 
If the Suggested Delta in the File Part Checksum Request message was out of bounds, the Office will adjust the value.  The previous Offset 
values are decremented by the Suggested Delta, aligned on the delta boundaries.  
For example, if the Offset value in the 02102 message is 100,000 and the Suggested Delta is 4096, then the following Byte Count values would be 
used for generating the MD5 checksums: 
- 73,728 (0x12000) 
- 77, 824 (0x13000) 
- 81,920 (0x14000) 
- 86, 016 (0x15000) 
- 90,112 (0x16000) 
- 94,208 (0x17000) 
- 98,304 (0x18000) 
- 100,000 (0x186a0) 
The Checksum Count list is ordered from smallest byte count first to largest byte count last.

The ‘ICD Version’ is the string that appears in the ‘Revision’ column of the Revision History table at the front of this document.  
Section 1.9 Data Dictionary contains the list of vendor codes and system codes. 
The components indicated in this message will be populated in the same order as they are listed in the Vendor Component Data Dictionary.  The 
faults will be listed according to the order that they were detected, with the most recent being listed first.  This message is limited to 255 total 
faults, which are allocated on a first come first served basis.  Once the 255 fault limit has been reached, each additional component will populate 
the ‘Number of Active Faults’ field with zero.  If there are no active faults to report for a component the ‘Number of Active  Faults’ field is populated 
with zero for that component.  The faults are independently reported from each source and are not merged (i.e. the same fault repo rted from all 
three PTC CPU slices would result in three entries in this message). 
Refer to the Onboard Segment Requirement Specification for requirements on when the message is sent. 
See the Data Dictionary for mapping between the ‘Locomotive State Summary’ and ‘Locomotive State’ fields.

[Constraints] 
[Unique error handling requirements] 
[Design notes] 
The ‘ICD Version’ is the string that appears in the ‘Revision’ column of the Revision History table at the front of this docu ment. 
Section 1.9 Data Dictionary contains the list of vendor codes and system codes. 
The ‘Locomotive State Summary’ and ‘Locomotive State’ fields represent the state of the on -board segment at the time that the Locomotive Fault 
Report (02087) message is created.  Due to the time filter applied, the on-board segment state may be different than when an individual Fault 
State Change occurred. 
See the Data Dictionary for mapping between the ‘Locomotive State Summary’ and ‘Locomotive State’ fields.  
The components indicated in this message will be populated in the same order as they are listed in the Vendor Component Data Dictionary.  The 
faults will be listed according to the order that they changes state, with the most recent being listed first.  This message is limited to 255 total 
faults, which are allocated on a first come first served basis.  Once the 255 fault limit has been reached, each additional component will populate 
the ‘Number of Fault State Changes’ field with zero.  If there are no active faults to report for a component the ‘Number of Fault State Changes’ 
field is populated with zero for that component.  The faults are independently reported from each source and are not merged (i.e. the same fault 
reported from all three PTC CPU slices would result in three entries in this message).

1.12 Filesets 
1.12.1 Fileset Definition 
EMP messages are utilized to transfer files from the office to the locomotive in groups called filesets.  A fileset is a grou p of files associated with a 
repository.  Fileset index files describe the contents of a fileset.  Each file within a fileset must have a unique name but different filesets may 
include the same file. 
1.12.2 Fileset Index File Field Format 
The fileset index file is a text file comprised of ASCII characters with line feed (LF) terminated lines; no carriage returns  (CR) are allowed.   The 
fileset index file is named <MD5>.fileset and the MD5 is calculated over the data in the fileset index file. 
Each fileset index file contains a series of fields that are used to identify data in the file.  The field format is shown be low using Unix symbol 
conventions. 
<Field Name> <Value> [Value . . .] 
Field Name 
A field name may be any alphanumeric combination including underscores.  Field names are used to identify following data.  
Value 
The value is the data that is associated with the field name.  Each field name may have one or more values.  A value may contain printable ASCII 
characters in the range of 0x20 to 0x7E inclusive.  When creating a fileset text file some values must be surrounded by double quotes as specified 
below.  When a value contains a double quote or a backslash (\) it must be preceded by a backslash (\). 
Below is a generic fileset index file.  The order of the fields must be observed for interoperable filesets.  The order of th e fields does not matter for 
non-interoperable filesets. 
name "<Name>" 
vers "<Version>" 
rrid "<Railroad SCAC>" 
repo "<Repository>" 
dest "<Destination>" 
file "<file name>" size=<file size in bytes> md5=<file MD5> 
file "<file name>" size=<file size in bytes> md5=<file MD5> 
.

-175- 
name <Name> 
The name field is required and occurs once in a fileset index file.  This field is a human readable name for the fileset.  The name value is 
surrounded by double quotes. 
vers <Version> 
The version (vers) field is required and occurs once in a fileset index file. The version value is surrounded by double quotes. 
rrid <Railroad SCAC> 
The railroad ID (rrid) field is required for the railroad specific repositories config and track.  It is not used for the software or common repositories.  
It occurs once in a fileset index file.  This field is populated with the SCAC of the railroad it represents .  The railroad SCAC value is surrounded by 
double quotes. 
repo <Repository> 
The repository (repo) field is required and occurs once in a fileset index file.  This field contai ns the repository associated with the fileset.  Valid 
repositories are listed in the data dictionary in section 1.9. The repository value is surrounded by double quotes. 
dest <Destination> 
The destination (dest) field is required only when the repository (repo) is set to software and occurs once in a fileset index file.  The destination 
specifies the platform on which the software is intended to run.  The destination value is surrounded by double quotes.  
file <file name> size=<file size in bytes> md5=<file MD5> 
The file field is used to describe a file in the fileset.  The fileset index file must contain one or more file fields.  The file name must be unique in the 
fileset.  The file name may only be the name of the file and should not include any directories.  The file name value is surrounded by double 
quotes.  The size field is populated with a decimal integer representing the file size in bytes.   The MD5 field is populated with the 32 lower case 
hexadecimal character MD5 calculated over the file.  For track files, the MD5 is calculated over the unencrypted version of the file.  
1.12.3 1.12.3 Interoperable Fileset Index Files 
For software and common configuration filesets from different interoperable railroads to be recognized as being the same, their version number 
and MD5 must match. 
The governing body assigned by the railroads (ITC) is responsible for maintaining the common configuration.  This includes de signating the name 
and version of each new common configuration fileset. 
1.12.4 1.12.4 Populating Fileset List Messages 
The Fileset List (01100) and Client Fileset List (02100) messages are populated with filesets to be downloaded from the railr oad Back Office..  The 
Fileset List (01100) message includes all filesets configured in the railroad Back Office for the Client groups.  The Client Fileset List (02100) 
message contains all filesets downloaded by the Client.
